response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but their background is centered on web platforms, product engineering, and developer tooling rather than hardware performance architecture. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator work, GPU architecture analysis, or deep-learning workload optimization — the core requirements of this principal-level role. Adjacent experience (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments, some embedded exposure) is too thin to bridge the gap.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Long, consistent engineering track record (18+ years) with a BS in Computer Science, meeting the years-of-experience bar
Some hardware-adjacent exposure: C/C++ instrumentation software at National Instruments and embedded/robotics work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design feedback, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product engineering, not performance architecture at the principal IC level
